items.headline: U.S. Equities Rebound Amid Tech Strength and Easing Tensions
items.summary: U.S. equities experienced a broad-based rebound today, driven by strong performance in technology and small-cap stocks. Market sentiment improved, with the VIX falling, though geopolitical tensions in the Middle East kept investors on edge. The rally followed hopes for renewed U.S.-Iran talks and cooling oil prices. Trading was characterized by selective buying in certain sectors and reduced exposure in others. Early economic data showed an increase in personal income and consumption expenditures for May 2026.

items.headline: Ampco-Pittsburgh Soars on Contract Wins
items.summary: Ampco-Pittsburgh ($AP) stock rocketed more than 75% following news of significant commercial contract wins and an expanding backlog.

items.headline: Alopexx Doubles Amid Market Enthusiasm
items.summary: Alopexx ($ALPX) saw its shares climb by 100% today, reflecting strong investor interest in the biotechnology sector.

items.headline: LegalZoom Plunges on CEO Departure and Revenue Cut
items.summary: LegalZoom ($LZ) shares fell 25.4% after the company announced the departure of its chief executive, Dan Wernikoff, and a cut to its full-year revenue forecast.

items.headline: Byrna Technologies Drops Significantly
items.summary: Byrna Technologies Inc. ($BYRN) experienced a notable decline of 33.67% today.

items.headline: Fed Officials Express Continued Inflation Concerns
items.summary: Minutes from the June 16-17 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting revealed that U.S. central bank officials remain highly concerned about inflation. Discussions highlighted a division among policymakers, with some favoring leaving rates unchanged and others leaning towards additional interest rate hikes if inflation persists.

items.headline: Technology and Small-Cap Stocks Lead Sector Rotation
items.summary: Today saw a clear recovery in risk appetite, with technology (XLK) and consumer discretionary (XLY) leading sector gains, rising 2.18% and 1.34% respectively. Financials (XLF) also performed well, gaining 1.04%. Conversely, energy (XLE) and consumer staples (XLP) lagged, dropping 1.40% and 1.41%. AI and semiconductor stocks were noted as key drivers in the broader market rally.

items.headline: May 2026 Economic Data Shows Income and Spending Growth
items.summary: The U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) reported that personal income increased by $181.6 billion (0.7 percent) in May 2026. Disposable personal income also rose by 0.7 percent, and personal consumption expenditures (PCE) saw a 0.7 percent increase.

items.headline: Delta Air Lines Reports Above-Expectation Earnings
items.summary: Delta Air Lines ($DAL) reported earnings of $1.56, surpassing expectations.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Remain a Market Factor
items.summary: Ongoing geopolitical tensions, particularly renewed hostilities in the Middle East, continue to be a source of caution for investors. Despite this, hopes for renewed U.S.-Iran talks and cooling oil prices contributed to today's equity rally.
